MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-12-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONERS IBRAHIM KAILEH, MAHA KAILEH RE-NOTICE OF HEARING OF PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE APPELLATE CASE DROLAPAS IS GOOD LAW AND IT BINDS THIS COURT BY ITS INTERPRETATION OF CIVIL CODE 1954.53(D)(2). THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT A REVIEW OF 1954.53(D) CASE LAW AND ITS LEGISLATIVE HISTORY INDICATES THAT THE TERM "RESIDE" IS USED IN A COLLOQUIAL, ORDINARY SENSE. MOREOVER, THE ALJ'S DECISION IS BOTH CONSISTENT WITH A COLLOQUIAL DEFINITION OF "RESIDE" AND REASONABLY SUPPORTED BY THE FACTS. THEREFORE, MORGAN IS A PROTECTED LAWFUL SUBLESSEE, AND HE RESIDED AT THE UNIT BEFORE JANUARY 1, 1996, PURSUANT TO 1954.53(D)(2). THE RENT BOARD DID NOT ABUSE ITS DISCRETION IN ITS DETERMINATION. ANY LEGAL OR FACTUAL DISPUTE CONCERNING RULE 6.14 IS MOOT. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501).

calendar.matters:
Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Goodman, Esq. and James Kraus, Esq. of Zacks, Freedman and Patterson, 415-956-8100, appearing for Ibrahim Kaileh, Plaintiff and moving party.

Wayne Snodgrass, Esq. of City and County of San Francisco, 415-554-4675, appearing for San Francisco Residential Rent, Respondent and opposing party.

Ora Prochovnick, Esq. of John F. Kennedy University, 925-969-3356, appearing for Morgan MacDonald, real property in interest.

Court reporter: Barrie Hart; CSR # 6954.; telephone: (415)328-6644; email: bhart@bayareatrialreporters.com .


ARGUED. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER IBRAHIM KAILEH, MAHA KAILEH Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is DENIED. The Court finds that the appellate case Drolapas is good law and it binds this Court by its interpretation of Civil Code 1954.53(d)(2). The Court further finds that a review of 1954.53(d) case law and its legislative history indicates that the term "reside" is used in a colloquial, ordinary sense. Moreover, the ALJ's decision is both consistent with a colloquial definition of "reside" and reasonably supported by the facts. Therefore, Morgan is a protected lawful sublessee, and he resided at the Unit before January 1, 1996, pursuant to 1954.53(d)(2). The Rent Board did not abuse its discretion in its determination. Any legal or factual dispute concerning Rule 6.14 is moot. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501).

Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-11-2019 Continued To Feb-13-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONERS IBRAHIM KAILEH, MAHA KAILEH'S Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is CONTINUED to February 13, 2019 at 9:30am in Dept. 501 on Court's own motion. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate

PETITIONERS IBRAHIM KAILEH, MAHA KAILEH'S Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is continued to January 11, 2019. Moving party is ordered to provide a copy of the Administrative Record, Hearing Transcript and ALJ decision no later than December 21, 2018 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
